data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers.** Both `nint` and `nuint` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  span  = stackalloc nint[4];   // ✅ works
Span<nuint> uspan = stackalloc nuint[2];  // ✅ works
```

Tested on .NET 11.0 preview — compiled and ran without issues. On this 64-bit system, `sizeof(nint)` is 8 bytes. This feature has been available since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class unmanaged types (which is the requirement for `stackalloc`).
